description: Centralised runtime configuration for run_full_project.ps1 and run_full_project_parallel.ps1. RunMode selects one of the named profiles below. paper_h40/paper_h60 are the custom9 horizon-ablation profiles (max_steps 40/60); their num_episodes (5000/3300) is scaled inversely with horizon so each training cell stays within the ~4.3h wall-clock of the paper profile (10000x20 == 5000x40 == 3300x60 step-units) and under the GitHub Actions 6h job cap. The flat custom9 training curve plateaus well before episode ~1000, so 3300-5000 episodes is ample to detect whether a longer horizon enables convergence. phase1 is the clean-lab KG-acceleration mode (lab1/lab2/lab3): num_episodes 3000 is ample for all three small state spaces (8/1024/2048) to converge AND plateau under their per-profile epsilon decays (0.9920/0.9960/0.9970), while running ~3x faster per cell than paper.

phase1_baseline.note: Factorial arm A (negative control): KG prior OFF, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF. KG is zeroed via stereo_prior_scale=0 + stereo_init_bonus=0, which triggers the supported H5 ablation in QLearner (zero Q-init AND zero greedy prior) so the ql_true arm becomes a true no-op overlay (≈ ql_false). Confirms the harness carries no built-in bias. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_only.note: Factorial arm C (HEADLINE): KG prior ON (stereo_prior_scale=1, stereo_init_bonus=15), PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF. With stereo as the matrix dimension this mode emits ql_false=arm A (tabula-rasa) and ql_true=arm C (KG prior only); the within-mode ql_true-vs-ql_false delta is the thesis anchor finding. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_only_ib5.note: Factorial arm C variant — identical to phase1_kg_only (KG prior ON, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF) but with the optimistic Q-init bonus lowered 15 -> 5. Tests the diagnosed lab3 hypothesis: that the +15 init bonus over-explores the ~2048-state lab3 space and delays first-goal. Compare its lab3 ql_true-vs-ql_false delta against phase1_kg_only (bonus=15); the lab2 win should be preserved. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_pbrs_only.note: Factorial arm B (KEY CONTROL): KG prior OFF (zeroed via H5 ablation), PBRS ON, adaptive-trust OFF. Its ql_false arm is the pure PBRS baseline. Cross-mode comparison against phase1_kg_only isolates the generic-shaping effect (A->B) and defends against the 'your speedup is just shaping' critique (C vs B). Same timing as phase1.

phase1_full.note: Factorial arm D (full stack): KG prior ON, PBRS ON, adaptive-trust ON. Equivalent to the default phase1 learning block; declared explicitly for symmetry with the other factorial arms. ql_true=arm D, ql_false=arm B. Same timing as phase1.

phase1_kg_xzone.note: KG-X arm (Part B lever): identical to phase1_kg_only (KG prior ON, PBRS OFF, adaptive-trust OFF) plus the cross-zone structural exploration prior cross_zone_bonus=3.0. Tests the lab3 spillage hypothesis: the KG declares WHICH components spill into the neighbour zone (ws:WeakOpticalCoupling in building_3_complex.ttl) and this small optimistic Q-init head-start nudges the agent to TRY the cross-zone lever, after which RL learns the true (sub-rank) magnitude. Compare its lab3 ql_true-vs-ql_false first-goal/coverage delta against phase1_kg_only (no xzone). Same timing as phase1.

phase2.description: Phase 2 fault-detection / blacklist / re-learn (run_phase2_adapt.ps1 + .github/workflows/phase2.yml). Each adapt profile injects one or more component faults into the named parent clean lab, warm-loads that lab's clean Phase-1 Q-table (qtable_final_stereotypes_<bool><clean_source_suffix>.csv), then runs the adapt agent (taskAdapt) which detects each defect via the KG Expected-vs-Actual check, blacklists BOTH polarities of every broken component (iteratively), and re-learns over the survivors. Recovery = reconverge_episode - detect_episode (lower is better; the headline compares ql_true < ql_false). Single-fault (f1*) and multi-fault (f2*) variants span lab1/lab2/lab3 complexities and {dead, inverted} fault modes. Only CAUSES lamps/spotlight are injected; healthy Mediates blinds are excluded from detection. lab1_f1dead is degenerate (single actuator; the blacklist guard preserves the last actuator) so it is a detection-only showcase with recovery=N/A. Parent ports/flows are reused because only one faulty variant runs at a time.
